name: hf-ward-readability
transcript: Registrar: Overnight she was less breathless and there was no further chest pain. Nurse: Telemetry showed brief atrial fibrillation overnight, now back in sinus rhythm. Doctor: Weight is down 1.2 kg, urine output was good, and fluid balance was negative 1.4 litres. Doctor: Blood pressure 108 over 64, heart rate 78, oxygen saturation 96 percent on room air, afebrile. Doctor: JVP is mildly elevated, bibasal crackles have improved, and there is only trace ankle oedema now. Doctor: Creatinine is stable, potassium is 4.2, troponin is flat, and yesterday's echo showed reduced LV systolic function with EF around 35 percent. Doctor: Overall this looks like improving decompensated HFrEF. Doctor: Continue IV furosemide today, monitor renal function and electrolytes, continue bisoprolol, and consider discharge in 24 to 48 hours if she keeps improving.

name: chest-pain-acs-readability
transcript: Doctor: Chest pain has improved since admission. Serial troponins have remained flat and there are no dynamic ECG changes. Blood pressure is stable and she is pain free currently. Doctor: Continue telemetry overnight, repeat ECG and troponins if recurrent pain, and discuss with cardiology if symptoms recur.

name: af-review-readability
transcript: Doctor: Brief atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response overnight. She is now back in sinus rhythm and feels better. Potassium is 4.3 and magnesium is 1.1. Continue bisoprolol, continue telemetry, and review anticoagulation with the consultant team.

name: consultant-letter-readability
transcript: Consultant: Referred for assessment of exertional chest pressure for the past three weeks. Symptoms improve with rest. Associated dyspnoea and intermittent left arm heaviness. ECG and blood work were arranged today, with stress testing to follow. Overall this is concerning for possible angina. Follow up after stress test results.

name: discharge-summary-readability
transcript: Doctor: Admitted with decompensated HFrEF and fluid overload. Improved with IV diuresis. Echo showed EF around 35 percent. Discharge diagnoses are decompensated HFrEF and brief paroxysmal atrial fibrillation. Continue furosemide and bisoprolol, arrange cardiology follow up, and repeat renal function and electrolytes after discharge. Return if worsening breathlessness, chest pain, palpitations, or recurrent fluid overload symptoms.

name: sparse-ward-round-conservative
transcript: Doctor: Slept okay. No chest pain. Mild breathlessness on walking to the toilet. Blood pressure okay. Creatinine stable. Continue current treatment and review tomorrow.

name: messy-multi-speaker-ward-round
transcript: Nurse: Overnight no acute events, telemetry mostly sinus. Patient: Still a bit short of breath getting up but no chest pain. Doctor: BP okay, sats 95 on room air, creatinine stable. Nurse: Weight down about one kilo from yesterday. Doctor: Keep current diuretic for today, repeat U and E tomorrow, review again tomorrow.

name: discharge-with-gaps-conservative
transcript: Doctor: Admitted with chest pain. Symptoms settled. Serial troponins were negative and ECG showed no dynamic change. Discharge diagnosis is non-cardiac chest pain. GP follow up advised. Return if recurrent chest pain.
